attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Orchestrates the hybrid simulator build: QEMU 9.x + vsim + TCG plugin.
# Run subcommands individually, or run with no args to build everything.

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
QEMU_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_qemu"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
PLUGIN_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/qemu_plugin"
QEMU_BUILD="$QEMU_DIR/build"
VSIM_BUILD="$VSIM_DIR/build"

JOBS="$(nproc)"

c_step()  { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m \033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
c_ok()    { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
c_err()   { printf '\033[1;31m[err]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require_dir() {
  if [[ ! -d "$1" ]]; then
    c_err "missing $1 - did you run \`git submodule update --init --recursive\`?"
    exit 1
  fi
}

build_qemu() {
  require_dir "$QEMU_DIR"
  c_step "QEMU: configure (if needed)"
  if [[ ! -f "$QEMU_BUILD/build.ninja" && ! -f "$QEMU_BUILD/Makefile" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "$QEMU_BUILD"
    (cd "$QEMU_BUILD" && ../configure \
      --target-list=riscv64-softmmu \
      --enable-plugins \
      --disable-werror)
  fi
  c_step "QEMU: build qemu-system-riscv64"
  if [[ -f "$QEMU_BUILD/build.ninja" ]]; then
    ninja -C "$QEMU_BUILD" qemu-system-riscv64
  else
    make -C "$QEMU_BUILD" -j"$JOBS" qemu-system-riscv64
  fi
  c_ok "QEMU built: $QEMU_BUILD/qemu-system-riscv64"
}

build_vsim() {
  require_dir "$VSIM_DIR"
  c_step "vsim: configure (if needed)"
  if [[ ! -f "$VSIM_BUILD/CMakeCache.txt" ]]; then
    cmake -S "$VSIM_DIR" -B "$VSIM_BUILD" -G Ninja \
      -DVSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON \
      -DVSIM_QEMU_ROOT="$QEMU_DIR"
  fi
  c_step "vsim: build"
  cmake --build "$VSIM_BUILD" -j"$JOBS"
  c_ok "vsim built: $VSIM_BUILD/vsim"
}

build_plugin() {
  require_dir "$PLUGIN_DIR"
  c_step "plugin: build libhybrid_handoff.so"
  make -C "$PLUGIN_DIR" QEMU_ROOT="$QEMU_DIR"
  c_ok "plugin built: $PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"
}

run_tests() {
  require_dir "$VSIM_BUILD"
  c_step "ctest: hybrid-e2e label"
  (cd "$VSIM_BUILD" && ctest -L hybrid-e2e --output-on-failure)
}

clean_all() {
  c_step "clean: remove build artifacts"
  rm -rf "$QEMU_BUILD" "$VSIM_BUILD"
  rm -f  "$PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"
  c_ok "cleaned"
}

usage() {
  cat <<EOF
usage: $(basename "$0") [qemu|vsim|plugin|test|clean|all]

  (no args)  same as 'all'
  qemu       configure + build hybrid_qemu/build/qemu-system-riscv64
  vsim       configure + build hybrid_vsim/build/vsim (VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON)
  plugin     build hybrid_vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so
  test       ctest -L hybrid-e2e (in vsim build dir)
  clean      remove both build dirs and the plugin .so
  all        qemu, then vsim, then plugin (in this order)
EOF
}

cmd="${1:-all}"
case "$cmd" in
  qemu)   build_qemu ;;
  vsim)   build_vsim ;;
  plugin) build_plugin ;;
  test)   run_tests ;;
  clean)  clean_all ;;
  all)    build_qemu; build_vsim; build_plugin ;;
  -h|--help|help) usage ;;
  *)      c_err "unknown command: $cmd"; usage; exit 2 ;;
esac
---
# hybrid_sim

Workspace for the Andes hybrid simulator: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz) + vsim
(SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) sharing memory and architectural state
across a magic-CSR boundary.

Two submodules:

- `hybrid_qemu/` - QEMU fork hosting the TCG plugin
- `hybrid_vsim/` - vsim sources, including `hybrid/qemu_plugin/` (plugin source)

## Quickstart

```sh
git clone --recursive https://gitea.andestech.com/nick/hybrid_sim
cd hybrid_sim
./build.sh           # qemu, vsim, plugin (in that order)
./build.sh test      # ctest -L hybrid-e2e
```

Subcommands: `qemu`, `vsim`, `plugin`, `test`, `clean`. Run `./build.sh -h`
for the full list.

## Host dependencies

- C/C++ toolchain (clang preferred), CMake >= 3.20, ninja
- Python 3
- glib-2.0 dev (for the QEMU plugin)
- pkg-config
- Verilator (auto-detected; set `VSIM_VERILATOR_EXECUTABLE` if needed)
- libraries QEMU itself needs (pixman, slirp, etc. - see `hybrid_qemu/README.rst`)

## Outputs

After `./build.sh`:

- `hybrid_qemu/build/qemu-system-riscv64`
- `hybrid_vsim/build/vsim`
- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build hybrid_qemu out-of-tree under build/qemu/.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
SRC="$ROOT/hybrid_qemu"
BUILD="$ROOT/build/qemu"

if [[ ! -x "$SRC/configure" ]]; then
  echo "missing $SRC/configure - run 'git submodule update --init --recursive'" >&2
  exit 1
fi

mkdir -p "$BUILD"

if [[ ! -f "$BUILD/build.ninja" && ! -f "$BUILD/Makefile" ]]; then
  (cd "$BUILD" && "$SRC/configure" \
    --target-list=riscv64-softmmu \
    --disable-werror \
    --disable-libnfs \
    --disable-werror)
fi

make -C "$BUILD" CFLAGS="-Wno-int-conversion -Wno-error=redundant-decls" -j64

1	#!/bin/bash
2	
3	# VSim Workspace Build Script
4	# This script builds released vsim image tar file
5	
6	set -e
7	
8	# arguments to docker targets
9	DOCKER_TARGET="$1"
10	if [[ ! "$DOCKER_TARGET" =~ ^(dev|runtime|bin|debug)$ ]]; then
11	    echo "Error: Invalid argument '$1'"
12	    echo "Usage: $0 {dev|runtime|bin|debug}"
13	    echo "  dev     - Development environment"
14	    echo "  runtime - Runtime environment"
15	    echo "  bin     - Binary only"
16	    echo "  debug   - debug version"
17	    exit 1
18	fi
19	
20	# Generate a version file
21	tag=$(git describe --tags --always)
22	echo "vsim version : " "$tag"
23	
24	if [[ "$DOCKER_TARGET" == "bin" ]]; then
25	    podman build --build-arg VERSION="$tag" --output=type=local,dest=andescycle-bin-"$tag" .
26	else
27	    # build image using docker or podman, archive build results
28	    podman build --target "$DOCKER_TARGET" --build-arg VERSION="$tag" -t andescycle-"$DOCKER_TARGET":"$tag" .
29	    podman save localhost/andescycle-"$DOCKER_TARGET":"$tag" | gzip > andescycle-"$DOCKER_TARGET"-"$tag".tar.gz
30	fi
31	
32	
33	echo "✅ Build complete"
34

file.content:
#!/bin/bash

# VSim Workspace Build Script
# This script builds released vsim image tar file

set -e

# arguments to docker targets
DOCKER_TARGET="$1"
if [[ ! "$DOCKER_TARGET" =~ ^(dev|runtime|bin|debug)$ ]]; then
    echo "Error: Invalid argument '$1'"
    echo "Usage: $0 {dev|runtime|bin|debug}"
    echo "  dev     - Development environment"
    echo "  runtime - Runtime environment"
    echo "  bin     - Binary only"
    echo "  debug   - debug version"
    exit 1
fi

# Generate a version file
tag=$(git describe --tags --always)
echo "vsim version : " "$tag"

if [[ "$DOCKER_TARGET" == "bin" ]]; then
    podman build --build-arg VERSION="$tag" --output=type=local,dest=andescycle-bin-"$tag" .
else
    # build image using docker or podman, archive build results
    podman build --target "$DOCKER_TARGET" --build-arg VERSION="$tag" -t andescycle-"$DOCKER_TARGET":"$tag" .
    podman save localhost/andescycle-"$DOCKER_TARGET":"$tag" | gzip > andescycle-"$DOCKER_TARGET"-"$tag".tar.gz
fi


echo "✅ Build complete"

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build vsim inside the env-stage container image.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"

if [[ ! -f "$VSIM_DIR/Dockerfile" ]]; then
  echo "missing $VSIM_DIR/Dockerfile - run 'git submodule update --init --recursive'" >&2
  exit 1
fi

cd "$VSIM_DIR"

docker build --target env -t vsim-env:latest .
docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/work" -w /work localhost/vsim-env:latest ./build.sh

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build vsim inside the env-stage container image.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"

if [[ ! -f "$VSIM_DIR/Dockerfile" ]]; then
  echo "missing $VSIM_DIR/Dockerfile - run 'git submodule update --init --recursive'" >&2
  exit 1
fi

cd "$VSIM_DIR"

content.text: Root cause: your shell has `alias docker=podman`, but aliases don't expand in non-interactive scripts, so the script invokes the real `/usr/bin/docker` and you lack docker-group access. Switching to `podman` directly (which the repo's `build-docker.sh` also uses).

docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/work" -w /work localhost/vsim-env:latest ./build.sh
input.new_string:

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build vsim inside the env-stage container image.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"

if [[ ! -f "$VSIM_DIR/Dockerfile" ]]; then
  echo "missing $VSIM_DIR/Dockerfile - run 'git submodule update --init --recursive'" >&2
  exit 1
fi

cd "$VSIM_DIR"

docker build --target env -t vsim-env:latest .
docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/work" -w /work localhost/vsim-env:latest ./build.sh

content.text: Re-run `./build_vsim.sh`. If you'd rather keep the `docker` command, the alternative is `sudo usermod -aG docker $USER` then re-login - but podman is rootless and matches the existing repo convention.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Driver: build qemu + vsim, then add build dirs to PATH.
# Source this script to affect your current shell's PATH:
#   source ./setup.sh
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
QEMU_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/build/qemu"
VSIM_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim/build"

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m \033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
err()  { printf '\033[1;31m[err]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

step "1/2 building qemu"
"$ROOT/build_qemu.sh"
ok "qemu -> $QEMU_BIN_DIR/qemu-system-riscv64"

step "2/2 building vsim"
"$ROOT/build_vsim.sh"
ok "vsim -> $VSIM_BIN_DIR/vsim"

step "PATH: prepending build dirs"
case ":$PATH:" in
  *":$QEMU_BIN_DIR:"*) ;;
  *) export PATH="$QEMU_BIN_DIR:$PATH" ;;
esac
case ":$PATH:" in
  *":$VSIM_BIN_DIR:"*) ;;
  *) export PATH="$VSIM_BIN_DIR:$PATH" ;;
esac
ok "PATH updated (this shell only)"

if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-}" == "${0:-}" ]]; then
  err "script was executed, not sourced - PATH change is local to this subshell"
  err "to update your shell, run: source ${BASH_SOURCE[0]}"
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Driver: build qemu + vsim, then add build dirs to PATH.
# Source this script to affect your current shell's PATH:
#   source ./setup.sh
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
QEMU_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/build/qemu"
VSIM_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim/build"

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m \033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
err()  { printf '\033[1;31m[err]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

step "1/2 building qemu"
"$ROOT/build_qemu.sh"
ok "qemu -> $QEMU_BIN_DIR/qemu-system-riscv64"

step "2/2 building vsim"
"$ROOT/build_vsim.sh"
ok "vsim -> $VSIM_BIN_DIR/vsim"

step "PATH: prepending build dirs"
case ":$PATH:" in
  *":$QEMU_BIN_DIR:"*) ;;
  *) export PATH="$QEMU_BIN_DIR:$PATH" ;;
esac
case ":$PATH:" in
  *":$VSIM_BIN_DIR:"*) ;;
  *) export PATH="$VSIM_BIN_DIR:$PATH" ;;
esac
ok "PATH updated (this shell only)"

if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-}" == "${0:-}" ]]; then
  err "script was executed, not sourced - PATH change is local to this subshell"
  err "to update your shell, run: source ${BASH_SOURCE[0]}"
fi
